danger_signals.visual_form: Red-colored symbiote appearance (Carnage character model), demonic visual traits on Demogoblin, six-armed deformity on Doppelganger
danger_signals.meaning: Active criminal threat; symbiote corruption; supernatural corruption; biological mutation

danger_signals.visual_form: Sewer environments as faction stronghold; crowds of inspired criminals filling levels
danger_signals.meaning: Organized criminal structure; widespread threat expansion; infection of city zones

danger_signals.visual_form: Character introductions of Shriek (second-in-command visual), Demogoblin (demonic transformation), Doppelganger (monstrous duplicate), Carrion (degradation visual state)
danger_signals.meaning: Escalating threat level; hierarchical enemy structure; diverse villain capabilities

danger_signals.visual_form: Combat positioning and health system during confrontations with Carnage's team

safety_signals.visual_form: Summoned allied heroes appearing via collected power-up items (Captain America, Black Cat, Iron Fist, Cloak and Dagger, Deathlok, Morbius, Firestar)
safety_signals.meaning: Temporary safety through backup; external aid availability; restoration of power

safety_signals.visual_form: Two playable characters working together despite rivalry tension
safety_signals.meaning: Temporary truce; united front against greater threat; ideological compromise

safety_signals.visual_form: Level progression mechanics allowing escape and regrouping
safety_signals.meaning: Tactical withdrawal possible; not cornered; time to prepare counteroffensive

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Carnage symbiote visual signature; demonic/corrupted appearance
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Shriek (sonic-capable visual), Demogoblin (demonic horns/wings), Doppelganger (six-armed mutation), Carrion (degradation/decay state)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Sewers as faction base; criminal-infested city zones; urban decay areas

faction_indicators.color_identity: Red and blue (Spider-Man); Black and white (Venom symbiote)
faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Spider emblem; Venom's aggressive alien symbiote form
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: City streets as patrol/confrontation zones; temporary alliance stronghold

faction_indicators.color_identity: Multiple (Captain America's red/white/blue, Black Cat's white, Iron Fist's green/yellow, etc.)
faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Individual hero insignia and costume designs; summoned via collected power-up items
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Not permanently stationed; appear via summoning mechanics

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Demonic power transformation visual (becoming Demogoblin after rejection)
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Hobgoblin → Demogoblin transformation indicates faction rejection leading to corruption

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Pre-game: Eddie Brock (Venom) released from prison, promises redemption
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Prison setting in narrative; Venom symbiote present but supposedly controlled
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Venom hesitant about commitment to good; Spider-Man suspicious

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Crisis initiation: Symbiote transfers to Cletus Kasady, becomes Carnage
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Red symbiote appearance; violent transformation visual; escape sequence
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Carnage immediately establishes criminal empire; Spider-Man and Venom forced into temporary alliance

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Threat escalation: Carnage recruits criminal team
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Multiple villain character models appearing; each with distinct visual corruption signature; sewer stronghold visualization
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Carnage rejects Hobgoblin (triggering Demogoblin transformation); actively hires other villains; demonstrates power hierarchy

world_state_to_visual.world_state: City under attack: Carnage launches criminal offensive
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Urban environments filled with hostile crowds; level design shows infiltration/occupation; danger visuals escalate
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Hundreds of inspired criminals fill levels; Spider-Man and Venom nearly killed; forced retreat

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Summoned allied heroes appear via collected power-ups; visual variety in hero roster; cutscenes show collaboration
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Previous allies recruited; coordinated approach to final confrontation

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Boss battle arena; Carnage visual state changes during combat phases; retreat into sewers (not complete destruction)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Symbiote escapes despite villain defeat; ambiguous ending suggests ongoing threat; Venom leaves before reply

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Escaped symbiote visual (still active); Venom character departure before reconciliation
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Spider-Man expresses hope for future cooperation but uncertainty remains; threat is suppressed but not eliminated

environmental_storytelling.example: Sewers established as Carnage's criminal empire stronghold; urban streets show infiltration/occupation by hired criminals
Hobgoblin's rejection leading to demonic transformation (Demogoblin); Carrion shown as degrading clone; Doppelganger as restrained six-armed abomination
Cutscenes feature semi-animated versions of art directly from the source comic story arc; visual continuity between game and narrative source material
Hundreds of inspired criminals filling levels creates visual sense of overwhelming force and city occupation without dialogue
Carnage's team hierarchy shown through visual distinctiveness: Shriek as second-in-command, specialized villain types with unique silhouettes and color schemes
Hidden power-ups that summon specific Marvel heroes establish that hero network exists within game world; location specificity suggests planned resistance movement

entity_behavioral_tells.state: Released from prison with conditional promise
entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Hesitant, ambiguous about commitment to good; willing to fight Carnage but unclear on long-term allegiance

entity_behavioral_tells.state: Suspicious of Venom; forced into partnership
entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Initiates confrontation with Venom; pragmatically accepts alliance against greater threat; expresses hope for future cooperation at end

Immediately establishes criminal empire; evenly matches Venom in direct combat; demonstrates leadership through selective recruitment (rejects Hobgoblin, hires Shriek, Demogoblin, Doppelganger, Carrion); initiates city-wide attack; escapes final defeat

entity_behavioral_tells.state: Attempts to join Carnage's criminal empire
entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Rejected by Carnage; corruption/transformation to demonic Demogoblin state follows rejection; suggests faction power dynamics visible through character transformation
Accepted into leadership role; visual positioning suggests authority under Carnage

entity_behavioral_tells.state: Six-armed Spider-Man reproduction; found in city
entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Initially restrained/controlled by Carnage; employed as part of villain team; visual deformity signals mutation/corruption

entity_behavioral_tells.state: Degrading Jackal clone; discovered by Carnage
entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Hired as final team member despite deteriorating condition; visual decay signals desperation in recruitment

entity_behavioral_tells.state: Hidden throughout levels; summoned via collected power-ups
entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Support Spider-Man or Venom depending on affinity; effectiveness varies by summoner; appear temporarily then depart

notes: Maximum Carnage is a story-driven beat-em-up that communicates world-state primarily through character introductions, environmental design (sewers vs. city streets), and visual faction identity. The red cartridge itself was a notable visual signal in the market (only non-black Genesis game). Narrative tension centers on temporary alliance (Spider-Man/Venom rivalry) versus existential threat (Carnage's syndicate). Visual communication of danger relies on villain distinctiveness, crowding mechanics, and corruption/transformation visuals. Safety signals emerge through summoning mechanics and hero team assembly, suggesting a resistance network. The ambiguous ending (Carnage escapes, Venom's uncertain commitment) maintains danger signal even in victory.
sources_used: Wikipedia: Spider-Man and Venom: Maximum Carnage
